key_insight: Plastic deformation of asperities can be modeled by transforming the surface power spectrum, enabling updated contact-mechanics predictions for rough metal surfaces after squeezing.
engineering_relevance: This matters for predicting contact stiffness, electrical/thermal contact resistance, and interfacial fluid flow in metal contacts where roughness evolves plastically.
method: Derive an effective power spectrum for plastically deformed surfaces.
Use the modified spectrum within Persson contact mechanics theory.
Compare predicted height topography with experimental steel-on-steel data.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports comparison of the model’s predicted topography against experiments by Yusof and Ripin on a smooth steel surface pressed against a rough steel surface.
watch_item: The approach may depend on how well the plasticity-modified spectrum represents different materials and surface classes, especially cases that become strongly skewed after deformation.
